tasks.direction_relation_note: Implements the next concrete slice from the supersession roadmap: Slice 1 capability detection and mode skeleton so parallel mode can enter as a supervisor-first shell state before any agent launch.
tasks.title: Add parallel mode readiness snapshot and shell entry skeleton
tasks.description: Introduce the first operator-visible supersession slice by adding explicit parallel mode entrypoints, durable readiness checks for git/worktree/push/gh/planning, and a placeholder supersession prepare surface that reports ready, degraded, or blocked state without disturbing normal-mode session browsing while parallel mode is off.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session via commit 1d193d8 and PR #252 after adding the readiness snapshot, `:parallel` entry commands, supersession overlay skeleton, and verification with cargo build/test.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the control-tower placeholder slice by shipping the read-only supervisor snapshot model and multi-panel supersession board, which now becomes the base surface for later runtime lifecycle wiring.
tasks.title: Build supervisor board with roster, pool, and distributor placeholders
tasks.description: Shipped the read-only supervisor snapshot model and supersession board layout with Capabilities, Pool Board, Agent Roster, Selected Detail, Distributor, and Queue panels plus shell-tail summaries, leaving runtime lifecycle and queue data binding for later follow-up slices.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session via commit 019c820 and PR #253 after adding the read-only supervisor snapshot model, multi-panel supersession board, and shell-tail summaries with cargo build/test verification.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Promoted as the next executable slice after the latest main-session reply closed non-merged slot operator recovery by verification only, leaving the ledger-owned completion refresh boundary as the clearest remaining runtime contract needed before supersession can safely advance official assignments after agent completion.
tasks.title: Define serialized completion refresh contract for official ledger updates
tasks.description: Implement the planning-facing completion payload and supervisor-side reported-versus-official state boundary so agent completion stays non-official until one serialized hidden planning worker refresh succeeds, and new assignment remains blocked while refresh is running or failed.
tasks.priority_reason: With pool recovery, roster projection, and distributor automation now all confirmed done, this is the next clear executable slice: it is dependency-ready, directly advances the planning-authority direction, and avoids leaving builtin next-task stalled behind proposal-only work.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the first pool runtime slice by replacing placeholder pool data with git/worktree-backed reconcile snapshots, deterministic akra pool-root discovery, and cleanup-aware slot-state classification on the supervisor board.
tasks.title: Implement akra pool reconcile and slot lease cleanup state
tasks.description: Shipped the first GitWorkspacePort-backed pool runtime slice that reconciles the default akra slots, computes deterministic pool-root naming, classifies missing, idle, awaiting_cleanup, blocked, and unavailable states from git/worktree inspection, and caches supervisor snapshots in the TUI so pool inspection does not rerun on every render.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session via commit 46ae69e and PR #254 after adding git/worktree-backed pool reconcile, cleanup-aware slot classification, TUI snapshot caching, and cargo build/test verification.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the next pool execution slice by splitting read-only inspect from mutating reconcile and adding actual akra baseline creation, missing slot provisioning, and merged-slot cleanup execution under the supervisor refresh path.
tasks.title: Implement akra slot provisioning and cleanup execution flow
tasks.description: Shipped the mutating akra reconcile flow so `:parallel on` and refresh can create the local akra baseline branch, provision missing default slots, clean merged agent branches, and reset or delete reusable slots back toward idle without manual filesystem intervention.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session via commit 904f695 and PR #255 after adding mutating akra reconcile, slot provisioning, merged-slot cleanup execution, and cargo build/test verification.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed the durable lease lifecycle slice by adding persisted slot lease records, `leased/running/cleanup_pending` transitions, and cleanup-state projection so the akra pool can now track ownership and merged cleanup readiness across refreshes.
tasks.title: Persist akra slot leases and running-state transitions
tasks.description: Shipped persisted akra slot lease records under `.leases/*.json`, lifecycle transition APIs for `leased`, `running`, and `cleanup_pending`, guardrails for branch/worktree ownership and akra integration checks, and cleanup inspection updates that now surface durable lease-backed slot states instead of inferred projections.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session via commit 3cf56d1 and PR #256 after adding persisted lease storage, lifecycle transition APIs, cleanup-state inspection updates, service tests, and cargo build/test verification.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed per the latest main-session verification: handoff launch now acquires slot leases before stream start, transitions matching leases to running on `TurnStarted`, releases clean slots on failed start, and hands successful completion into the post-turn lifecycle APIs for cleanup-pending handling.
tasks.title: Wire agent session runtime into slot lease lifecycle APIs
tasks.description: The real agent and session runtime now drives `acquire_slot_lease`, `mark_slot_running`, failed-start release, and success-path cleanup-pending transitions from the turn-submission runtime and post-turn execution flow instead of relying on service-only helpers.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in the latest main-session reply with code-path verification across `turn_submission_runtime`, `stream_execution`, `post_turn_execution`, and `parallel_mode_service`, plus targeted tests covering leased launch, running transition, failed-start release, and success completion cleanup gating.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed by the same runtime verification that completed lease wiring: startup failure now takes the explicit release path and resets a clean leased slot back to idle instead of leaving recovery to ad hoc cleanup.
tasks.title: Add explicit lease release for bootstrap failure recovery
tasks.description: A dedicated failed-start release path now returns a clean leased slot back to idle when bootstrap or early runtime setup fails before durable running state, covering the bootstrap recovery gap that was previously tracked separately.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in the latest main-session reply, which verified the bootstrap-failure release path with `release_workspace_slot_lease_after_failed_start_resets_clean_slot_to_idle` and confirmed the runtime now performs the explicit idle reset automatically.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed after the latest main-session reply verified the baseline already projects live supervisor roster data from pool context and lease or session detail state, so this control-tower slice no longer needs a separate implementation pass.
tasks.title: Wire supervisor roster to live agent lifecycle state
tasks.description: Replace static agent roster placeholders with runtime lifecycle projection that shows task ownership, slot identity, lease state, running duration, and latest summary for active and recently completed agents once actual agent/session execution is wired into the persisted lease lifecycle.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed by verification in the latest main-session reply: `parallel_mode_service` already builds the supervisor roster from live pool context and projects `starting`, `running`, and `cleanup_pending` states, with targeted service tests re-run and passing.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed after the latest main-session reply verified the baseline already implements the operator-recovery path for non-merged slot branches, including blocked recovery notices, awaiting-cleanup separation, and supervisor notice rendering without requiring additional code changes in this turn.
tasks.title: Handle operator recovery for non-merged slot branches
tasks.description: The baseline now stands verified as surfacing lease-less non-merged slot branches as explicit operator recovery or awaiting-cleanup states, with consistent reconcile-status and top-notice messaging rendered through the supersession overlay.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed by verification in the latest main-session reply: `parallel_mode_service.rs` already defines the operator-recovery cause and next action, projects lease-less non-merged branches as `Blocked / operator recovery`, keeps cleanup-ready lease-less branches in `AwaitingCleanup`, and re-ran the three targeted recovery tests successfully.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed after the latest main-session reply verified the baseline already binds the distributor snapshot to live queue records and session history, including selected-detail preference for the active queue head session when present.
tasks.title: Bind distributor queue and selected detail to runtime data
tasks.description: The supervisor snapshot now reads live distributor queue records and session history so the distributor and selected-detail panels reflect runtime queue membership, queue-head context, and active-session linkage instead of placeholders.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed by verification in the latest main-session reply: `parallel_mode_service` already projects live distributor queue data and prefers the active queue head when resolving selected runtime detail, and `cargo test build_supervisor_snapshot_prefers_active_distributor_queue_head_for_selected_detail -- --nocapture` passed without requiring further code changes.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed after the latest main-session reply verified the baseline already executes the single-consumer local distributor flow: `process_distributor_queue(...)` handles only the active head, blocks later items behind blocked or failed heads, integrates the head into local `akra`, and completes slot cleanup plus idle return.
tasks.title: Implement serial distributor queue-head handling and local akra integration
tasks.description: The baseline distributor flow now processes one commit-ready queue head at a time, preserves queue-head blocking semantics for later items, integrates successful heads into local `akra`, and returns the slot to idle only after cleanup succeeds.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed by verification in the latest main-session reply without new code changes: `parallel_mode_service.rs` already enforces single-head processing and local `akra` integration, and the targeted tests for commit-ready delivery and blocked-head queue retention both passed again.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed after the latest main-session reply verified that the baseline already handles distributor queue-head rebase, safe push, PR open-or-update, and integration-branch push sequencing, so this remote automation boundary no longer needs a separate implementation pass.
tasks.title: Add distributor rebase, push, and PR automation for queue heads
tasks.description: Extend the serial distributor from local-only `akra` integration into the remote automation path for one queue head at a time: detect stale source heads when `akra` advances, run explicit rebase and provenance refresh, push with safe lease semantics, open or update the PR, and surface capability degradation or rebase conflicts as blocked queue-head state instead of silently advancing.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed by verification in the latest main-session reply without new code changes: `parallel_mode_service.rs` already drives distributor heads through `pushing -> pr_pending -> merge_pending -> integrating`, performs `akra`-based rebase plus `--force-with-lease` push when the source SHA changes, and uses the GitHub automation port or adapter boundaries for push, PR ensure or inspect, integration push, and PR close; the targeted distributor tests were re-run and passed.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Tracked as an operational pool-baseline recovery slice so the local `/home/akra/codex-exec-loop` checkout matches merged PR #271 on `origin/prerelease` before further queue work resumes.
tasks.title: Resync local prerelease checkout to merged PR #271 baseline
tasks.description: Switch the local checkout back to `prerelease`, fast-forward it to merged PR #271 baseline commit `60151f0`, and preserve any preexisting local runtime-state edits safely outside the working tree so the main workspace returns to a clean queue-ready baseline.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in the latest main session reply after the local checkout was fast-forwarded to `60151f0`, `HEAD` and `origin/prerelease` were aligned, the worktree was confirmed clean, and the displaced runtime-state edits were preserved in `stash@{0}` (`resync-local-prerelease-baseline-2026-04-17`).

tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed after the latest main-session reply verified `stash@{0}` contains only one-off internal runtime-state snapshots, confirmed no source changes need to be replayed onto refreshed `prerelease`, and left only an operator retention decision about whether to keep or drop the preserved stash record.
tasks.title: Review preserved runtime-state stash after prerelease baseline resync
tasks.description: Inspected `stash@{0}` (`resync-local-prerelease-baseline-2026-04-17`) and confirmed the preserved diff is limited to obsolete internal runtime-state snapshots, so there is no product or source change that should be replayed on top of local `prerelease` commit `60151f0`.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in the latest main-session reply by reviewing `stash@{0}`, determining it should not be reapplied, and reducing the remaining follow-up to a separate operator decision about stash retention versus cleanup.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Split out from the completed stash review because the latest main-session reply leaves one non-implementation follow-up: decide whether the already-reviewed `stash@{0}` should remain as a preserved record or be dropped now that it is confirmed obsolete for code replay.
tasks.title: Decide whether to retain or drop obsolete resync stash
tasks.description: Make the operator-level retention decision for `stash@{0}` (`resync-local-prerelease-baseline-2026-04-17`): either keep the reviewed stash as a temporary audit record or explicitly drop it now that it has been confirmed unnecessary for restoring product code on top of `prerelease` commit `60151f0`.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply reduces the remaining follow-up to a judgment call about record retention rather than executable implementation work, so this should stay proposed until the operator chooses whether cleanup is desired.
